propositions: Removed env vars are silently ignored and fall through to defaults, causing surfaces like TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR to redirect the store to .tickets-tracker with no warning.
Removed TOML keys only get a generic unknown-key warning that names neither the replacement nor the removal, silently dropping security/policy behavior.
A RemovedInput tombstone registry should map retired env/cfg/file inputs to targeted migration errors naming old name, replacement, and removed-in version with nonzero exit.
RemovedInputError must subclass BaseException so it is not swallowed by tracker_dir's except ConfigError or MCP boot's broad except Exception handlers.
Env-var and file-presence tombstone checks must run at the top of load_config before the cache lookup so they fire on warm-cache hits in the long-running MCP server.
A CI golden-file anti-regression guard must fail if a recognized config key or env var is removed without a corresponding tombstone entry.
